meta.document_purpose: Complete specification for an AI assistant to fully understand, design and implement the IT Knowledge Graph application
changelog: v2.0.0–v2.5.0: See previous versions for full history

v2.6.0: last_quiz_score format defined, NodeTargetSelector useEffect sync, refreshWarnings() implemented, useBlocker replaced with hashchange guard for HashRouter, getNeighborIds() and getNode() fully defined, requestIdleCallback described honestly (delays start, still blocks), global view 150-node selection strategy defined (most connected), storage.js renameNode() added, ZIP scan progress specified separately from folder mode, dependency versions pinned, private fields stripped before AI payload, Phase 1a zipDirty clarification note, selectNode() quiz guard via getState() pattern shown, color null omitted from frontmatter, rename failure simulation method documented, getNode() defined
meta.how_to_use: Read entirely before writing any code. Respect phase order. When in doubt, refer to constraints.

project.name: IT Knowledge Graph
project.tagline: A graph-based second brain for IT learning, powered by local files and AI
project.core_philosophy: The app orchestrates files the user owns. If the app disappears, the knowledge remains.
known_limitations: Single tab usage only — concurrent tabs with the same folder may cause write conflicts.
File System Access API supported in Chrome and Edge only. Firefox/Safari → ZIP mode.
AI features require internet. All other features work offline.
No real-time collaboration — single-user by design.
useBlocker (React Router) is unreliable with HashRouter for the browser back button. The quiz navigation guard uses hashchange events instead — see quiz_navigation_guard.

fundamental_architecture_decision.principle: Markdown files on disk ARE the database. The app reads and writes them via the File System Access API.

example_file.content_example:
---
id: docker
label: Docker
category: logiciel
priority: high
status: learning
last_quiz_score: null
next_review: null
ease_factor: 2.5
interval: 1
createdAt: 2025-01-10T10:00:00Z
updatedAt: 2025-01-10T10:00:00Z
relations:
  - target: kubernetes
    type: depends_on
    weight: 0.9
  - target: linux
    type: uses
    weight: 0.7
resources:
  - type: documentation
    title: Docker Official Docs
    url: https://docs.docker.com
---

## What is Docker?
Docker is a containerization platform.

## Key Concepts
- **Image**: Read-only template
- **Container**: Running instance
- **Dockerfile**: Build script
- **Registry**: Image storage
example_file.color_field_note: color is OMITTED from frontmatter when null (the default). Only write color if user explicitly sets a custom value. matter.stringify omits undefined fields — set color to undefined (not null) in the data object before serializing.

dependencies.install_command: npm install react@^18 react-dom@^18 react-router-dom@^6.20 react-force-graph-2d@^1.25 d3@^7 gray-matter@^4 react-markdown@^9 remark-gfm@^4 idb-keyval@^6 jszip@^3 fuse.js@^7 zustand@^4

packages.name: react-force-graph-2d
packages.note: Pin minor version — API has had breaking changes between majors

packages.name: d3
packages.note: EXPLICIT dependency. react-force-graph-2d does not expose d3-force for direct import.

dependencies.pinning_rationale: All versions are pinned to a known-good major. Do not use 'latest' for any package — silent breaking changes will occur over time.

routing.reason: Required for GitHub Pages static hosting

react_strict_mode.instruction: DISABLE StrictMode — do not wrap App in React.StrictMode
react_strict_mode.reason: react-force-graph-2d double-initializes its physics simulation under StrictMode

handle_persistence.rule: FileSystemHandle is NOT JSON-serializable. Use IndexedDB via idb-keyval.

memory_limit.check:
const totalBytes = Object.values(zip.files).reduce((a, f) => a + (f._data?.uncompressedSize || 0), 0);
if (totalBytes > 20 * 1024 * 1024) showMemoryWarning((totalBytes/1024/1024).toFixed(1));

fallback_storage.dirty_guard: useBeforeUnload(zipDirty, editorDirty) — fires on BOTH states in any storage mode

color_serialization.rule: When building the data object for matter.stringify(), set color to undefined (not null) if the user has not set a custom color. matter.stringify omits undefined fields, preventing 'color: null' from appearing in every file.

two_pass.note: dirHandle.entries() returns a NEW independent AsyncIterableIterator on each call. Calling it twice for two passes is correct and safe — do not cache the first iterator.

zip_scan.note: JSZip loads the entire archive into memory at once. There is no streaming. Count is available immediately after loadAsync — no two-pass needed.

search.rebuild_rule: Rebuild index in useEffect watching nodes array. An outdated index omits newly created nodes.

proxy_url_missing_ui.location: Persistent banner at TOP of detail panel AI section, REPLACING both AI buttons
proxy_url_missing_ui.message: AI features not configured. Set VITE_AI_PROXY_URL in .env.local and restart.

slugify.collision_handling: Append numeric suffix: nodejs-2. Warn user.

rename_operation.handle_refresh: After deleting oldId.md, get a fresh handle: const freshHandle = await dirHandle.getFileHandle(newId + '.md'); then set node._handle = freshHandle; node._filename = newId + '.md'; before calling updateNodeInMemory().
rename_operation.failure_simulation_for_testing: To test rollback: temporarily wrap dirHandle.getFileHandle in a mock that throws after N calls. Example in tests: let callCount = 0; const origGet = dirHandle.getFileHandle.bind(dirHandle); dirHandle.getFileHandle = async (...args) => { if (++callCount > 2) throw new Error('Simulated failure'); return origGet(...args); };
steps: 2. Snapshot content of ALL files to be modified.
3. Write newId.md with updated id, label, updatedAt.
4. Rewrite each dependent file: replace oldId → newId in relations.
5. If ALL succeed: delete oldId.md. Refresh _handle (see handle_refresh above).
6. If ANY fail: delete newId.md if created, restore snapshots, show error.
7. updateNodeInMemory() with refreshed node. Never refreshGraph().

normalize_node.rule: Call on EVERY parsed node. Never use raw frontmatter directly.
normalize_node.implementation:
export function normalizeNode(raw) {
  return {
    id: raw.id || slugify(raw.label || 'unnamed'),
    label: raw.label || raw.id || 'Unnamed',
    category: raw.category || 'concept',
    color: raw.color || null,
    priority: ['low','medium','high'].includes(raw.priority) ? raw.priority : 'medium',
    status: ['learning','to_review','mastered'].includes(raw.status) ? raw.status : 'learning',
    last_quiz_score: raw.last_quiz_score || null,
    next_review: raw.next_review || null,
    ease_factor: (typeof raw.ease_factor === 'number' && !isNaN(raw.ease_factor)) ? raw.ease_factor : 2.5,
    interval: (typeof raw.interval === 'number' && !isNaN(raw.interval) && raw.interval > 0) ? raw.interval : 1,
    createdAt: raw.createdAt || new Date().toISOString(),
    updatedAt: raw.updatedAt || new Date().toISOString(),
    relations: Array.isArray(raw.relations) ? raw.relations : [],
    resources: Array.isArray(raw.resources) ? raw.resources : [],
    markdown_body: raw.markdown_body || '',
    _handle: raw._handle || null,
    _filename: raw._filename || ((raw.id || 'unknown') + '.md')
  };
}

graph_functions.description: All functions exported from graph.js. Used by graph_store and ai.js.

getNode.signature: getNode(nodeId: string, nodes: Node[]): Node | undefined
getNode.implementation: export const getNode = (nodeId, nodes) => nodes.find(n => n.id === nodeId);
getNode.note: Always pass the current nodes array from graph_store. Do not use a closure over stale state.

getNeighborIds.signature: getNeighborIds(nodeId: string, depth: number, links: Link[]): Set<string>
getNeighborIds.description: BFS traversal returning all neighbor IDs within the given depth. Treats links as undirected.

buildGraph.description: Build full in-memory graph from parsed nodes. Detects slug collisions.

graph_functions.buildLinks: Build link array from nodes.relations. Skip dangling (target not in node id set).
graph_functions.buildClusters: Group nodes by category. Each cluster: { category, node_ids, color }.

refreshWarnings.description: Recompute dangling_relation warnings from current in-memory nodes. Preserves parse_error and slug_collision warnings (those come from disk, not memory).
refreshWarnings.usage: Called after: fixAllDanglingRelations(), fixOneDanglingRelation(), deleteNode(). Pass current nodes and current warnings.

computeStaticForceLayout.description: Pre-compute static node positions using a fixed number of D3 force simulation ticks.
computeStaticForceLayout.honesty_note: requestIdleCallback delays the START of computation so the browser can paint a loading spinner first. However, the 300 D3 ticks themselves run synchronously and WILL block the main thread for ~50–300ms depending on node count. This is acceptable for < 100 nodes. For 100–150 nodes, reduce to 150 ticks to limit freeze duration. A Web Worker would be needed for true non-blocking behavior but is out of scope.

selectTopNodes.description: When global view has more than 150 nodes, select WHICH 150 to show.
selectTopNodes.strategy: Show the 150 most connected nodes (highest relation count). If a category filter is active, apply the filter first then take top 150 from the filtered set.

storage_interface.description: Unified interface routing to fs.js (folder) or zip.js (ZIP). ALL components use only this file.
storage_interface.created_in_phase: 1a (folder mode only). Updated in Phase 1b to add ZIP routing.
storage_interface.phase_1b_update_task: In Phase 1b, EXPLICITLY update storage.js to import zip.js and route based on storageMode. This is a required task — not optional.

interface.readAllNodes(): Returns { nodes, warnings, rawContentCache }. Routes to folder or ZIP scan.
interface.writeNode(node): verifyYamlRoundTrip() then write. Routes to fs.js or zip.js.
interface.deleteNode(id): Delete file. Routes to fs.js or zip.js.

renameNode(oldId, newId, newLabel, allNo.description: Orchestrates the full rename: snapshot, write new, rewrite dependents, delete old, refresh handle.
renameNode(oldId, newId, newLabel, allNo.note: renameNode() is in storage.js (not directly in graph_store) because it involves multi-file coordination that belongs in the storage layer. graph_store.renameNode() calls storage.renameNode() then updateNodeInMemory() with the result.
renameNode(oldId, newId, newLabel, allNo.returns: { renamedNode, updatedDependents } — the renamed node (with fresh _handle) and list of dependent nodes updated

node_color.rule: Computed at render time. NEVER auto-written to file. Only write if user explicitly sets custom color.
node_color.render: export const getNodeColor = (node) => node.color || CATEGORY_COLOR_MAP[node.category] || CATEGORY_COLOR_MAP.default;

node_frontmatter_schema.label: string required
node_frontmatter_schema.color: string | omitted — only present if user set a custom color explicitly
node_frontmatter_schema.priority: enum: low | medium | high — default medium
node_frontmatter_schema.status: enum: learning | to_review | mastered — default learning
node_frontmatter_schema.interval: number — SM-2 interval in days, default 1
node_frontmatter_schema.updatedAt: ISO date string — updated on every save
node_frontmatter_schema.relations: array of { target: string, type: string, weight: number 0–1 }. Types are plain strings — NOT a closed enum.
node_frontmatter_schema.resources: array of { type: enum, title: string, url: string }

in_memory_graph.links: { source: id, target: id, type: string, weight: number }[]
in_memory_graph.clusters: { category: string, node_ids: string[], color: string }[]
in_memory_graph.rawContentCache: Map<filename, rawString> — populated for parse-failed files only

incremental_update.rule: After any single-node write, use updateNodeInMemory / addNodeInMemory / removeNodeFromMemory. Call refreshWarnings() after operations that affect relations. NEVER call refreshGraph() for single writes.
incremental_update.refreshGraph_usage: Only on: initial load, folder/ZIP re-open, explicit user 'Reload' action.

global_view_mode.node_selection: Use selectTopNodes(nodes, links, 150, activeCategory). Shows most-connected nodes. Show warning if truncated.
global_view_mode.layout: computeStaticForceLayout() via requestIdleCallback. Show spinner during computation. Cache result in globalLayoutCache. Invalidate cache on any node mutation.
global_view_mode.react_force_graph_props: d3AlphaDecay={1} d3VelocityDecay={1} cooldownTicks={0} with fx/fy set from cache on each node

interactions.click_node: Check quizState before selectNode() — see quiz_navigation_guard.same_page_guard

dangling_relations.fix_one: Remove relation from file. writeNode() → updateNodeInMemory() → refreshWarnings().

fix_all.strategy: Best-effort. Never abort on partial failure.

parse_errors.show_raw_content: Read from rawContentCache.get(filename). If missing: 'Re-open folder to re-scan.'

graph_health_panel.empty_state: Green checkmark: 'Your knowledge graph is healthy.'

form_fields.label: Text input. Live slug preview below: 'Will be saved as: docker.md'
form_fields.color: Optional color picker. Cleared → undefined → omitted from frontmatter.

value_sync.rule: Use useEffect to synchronize inputText when the external value prop changes. Without this, the displayed label stays stale when a relation entry is edited from outside.

target_selector.on_change_trigger: onChange fires ONLY on: dropdown item selection (onMouseDown) OR input blur. NOT on every keystroke.

relations.type_field: Autocomplete with known defaults. Accepts any string.

form_fields.markdown_body: Textarea + toolbar. Char counter. Warning at 10,000 chars.

editor_view.dirty_tracking: editorDirty = true on any field change. False on successful save or confirmed cancel.

save_behavior.pre_save: verifyYamlRoundTrip(). color serialized as undefined if null.
save_behavior.rename_trigger: If new label produces different slug → storage.renameNode() flow.

scan_loading.folder_mode: Two-pass: count then parse. Live counter: 'Loading X / Y'.
scan_loading.zip_mode: Count available after loadAsync. Single pass with counter.

ai_features.phase_gate: Phase 4 MUST NOT start before ALL Phase 3 verifications pass.

score_to_last_quiz_score.rule: last_quiz_score is stored as a human-readable string '4/5', NOT as a float '0.8'.

quiz_state.answer_finality: Answers are FINAL on first click. Disable all option buttons for the current question immediately after any option is clicked.

transitions.generateQuiz: { nodeId, questions, currentIndex: 0, answers: new Array(N).fill(null), completed: false, score: null }
transitions.submitAnswer: answers[currentIndex] = answerIndex. If last: compute score, completed = true.
transitions.finishQuiz: sm2() → writeNode() with all 6 SM-2 fields + last_quiz_score → updateNodeInMemory() → resetQuiz()

quiz_navigation_guard.problem: useBlocker from React Router v6 has documented unreliable behavior with HashRouter — specifically the browser back button does not always trigger the block. useBlocker is designed for BrowserRouter.

route_changes.approach: Listen to window hashchange event and block it manually when quiz is in progress.
route_changes.modal: QuizBlockModal: 'Quiz in progress (Q X/Y). Leave and lose progress?' → [Continue Quiz] [Abandon Quiz]

same_page_guard.description: When user clicks a different node in the graph while quiz panel is open.
same_page_guard.pattern: Check quizState via useAiStore.getState() inside the component handler, before calling selectNode().

prompt_templates.note: ES6 template literals in actual code. Wrap in backticks.

summary.system: You are a technical educator. Generate structured pedagogical explanations in Markdown.

quiz.system: You are a technical quiz generator. Return ONLY raw JSON arrays. No markdown, no code fences.

quiz.min_content_message: Add more content (min 100 chars) before generating a quiz

cloudflare_worker.localhost_note: localhost entries allow dev access. Keep Worker URL private or remove localhost entries after development.

application_state.cross_store_pattern: Use StoreX.getState() inside actions or event handlers to read state from another store. Never import store hooks inside store files.

ui_store.removed: navigateSafely() — replaced by hashchange guard + same-page inline checks

development_phases.rule: Complete each phase fully before starting the next.

phase_1a_folder_core.goal: App opens a folder and displays a working graph. Core lib in place.
tasks: Run npm install command from dependencies section.
Setup React + Vite. Disable StrictMode with comment in main.jsx.
Set base in vite.config.js.
Create slugify.js.
Create graph.js: normalizeNode(), getNode(), getNeighborIds(), buildGraph() with collision detection, buildLinks(), buildClusters(), refreshWarnings(), computeStaticForceLayout(), selectTopNodes().
Create fs.js: two-pass folder scan with independent entries() calls, normalizeNode(), rawContentCache, IndexedDB handle persistence.
Create storage.js: folder mode only at this stage. writeNode() includes verifyYamlRoundTrip() and color→undefined serialization.
Create Zustand stores.
ScanLoader component with live counter.
GraphErrorBoundary.
react-force-graph-2d: cluster mode default, global mode with computeStaticForceLayout + spinner + cache.
useSingleTabGuard: handler before postMessage.
useBeforeUnload(zipDirty, editorDirty): zipDirty will be false in Phase 1a — this is expected and correct. The hook is created now so editorDirty protection works immediately.
5 sample .md files using slugify(). Verify 'TCP/IP' → 'tcpip'.
phase_1a_folder_core.done_when: Folder opens, scan shows counter, graph renders, global view layout is readable, GraphErrorBoundary works, storage.js ready.

phase_1b_zip_mode.goal: ZIP mode fully functional. storage.js updated.
tasks: Create zip.js: loadZip() with JSZip single-pass scan + memory check, exportZip().
UPDATE storage.js: import zip.js, route all operations by storageMode, add renameNode() orchestration, add loadZip(), exportZip(), isZipDirty().
ZIP badge + non-dismissible banner.
Welcome screen: two buttons if FSA supported, one if not.
Verify beforeunload fires for both zipDirty and editorDirty.
phase_1b_zip_mode.done_when: ZIP mode works. storage.js routes correctly. Memory warning shows. beforeunload covers both dirty states.

tasks: Node click handler checks quizState via useAiStore.getState() (quiz guard for Phase 4).
Detail panel with Markdown render.
Hover highlight/dim.
Cluster expand.
Relations list (skip dangling).
Fuse.js search — useEffect rebuilds on nodes change.
Top nav.
hashchange quiz guard listener registered in App.jsx (modal shown only when quiz active).
phase_2_interaction.done_when: Click, hover, search, read work. New nodes appear in search. hashchange listener in place.

phase_3_editing.goal: Full CRUD. Completely stable before Phase 4.
tasks: Editor: all fields, slug preview, Markdown preview, char counter.
NodeTargetSelector: useEffect value sync, onChange on blur/select only, ⚠️ for unresolved targets.
editorDirty tracking.
color serialized as undefined (not null) before matter.stringify.
verifyYamlRoundTrip() before every write.
writeNode() → updateNodeInMemory(). refreshWarnings() after relation-affecting ops.
Create node with collision detection.
storage.renameNode() wired to graph_store.renameNode(). _handle refreshed after success.
Delete with best-effort cascade.
Fix All dangling: best-effort + toast with counts.
ZIP export button.
List view, Dashboard, Graph Health panel.
phase_gate_verification: Create 'TCP/IP' → id='tcpip', filename='tcpip.md'
Create 'C++ Basics' → id='c-basics'
Create two nodes with same slug → collision suffix, Graph Health shows slug_collision
Edit node label 'HTTP: Protocol' → save succeeds or clear YAML error shown
Edit node → updatedAt changes, graph updates without full re-parse
color left empty → color field absent from written .md file
color set by user → color field written to .md file
Create node → immediately searchable (Fuse rebuilt)
Rename 'Docker' → 'Docker Engine': old file gone, new file created, relations updated, subsequent edit succeeds (handle refreshed)
Rename failure simulation (mock getFileHandle to fail after 2 calls) → rollback, no corrupt state
Delete with relations → Graph Health shows remaining dangling links
Fix All dangling → toast shows counts, Graph Health refreshed
Load .MD uppercase file → loaded
Load .md missing 'category' → normalizeNode fills default, no crash
Load .md with YAML error → Graph Health parse_errors shows it, Show raw content works
Add relation to non-existent node → ⚠️ in editor, Graph Health shows after save
Edit form → close tab without saving → beforeunload fires
ZIP: export → reimport → all fields intact including interval and ease_factor
Open NodeTargetSelector for a relation, change parent value externally → inputText updates
Switch to Global View (>5 nodes) → spinner shows, layout computes, result readable, no crash
Mutate node → globalLayoutCache = null → next Global View recomputes
phase_3_editing.done_when: ALL verifications pass. Zero known bugs.

tasks: Cloudflare Worker deployed with origin check + rate limiting.
Env vars wired.
VITE_AI_PROXY_URL missing → banner replaces AI buttons in detail panel.
callAI(): navigator.onLine check + TypeError catch. getAIErrorMessage() for user messages.
buildAIContext(): uses getNode() and getNeighborIds() with links join for weight. Strips _handle/_filename from payload.
generateSummary: ES6 template prompt, cache, loading state.
generateQuiz: ES6 template prompt, parseQuizResponse() with schema validation.
Disable 'Take Quiz' if markdown_body < 100 chars.
QuizState per interface. Answers final on click (options disabled immediately).
hashchange guard: blocks navigation when quiz active. Same-page guard: check useAiStore.getState().quizState in GraphView node click.
finishQuiz(): score = correctCount/total. last_quiz_score = '4/5' format. sm2() with defensive defaults. Write all 6 fields via writeNode() + updateNodeInMemory().
Depth slider (1–3), difficulty selector.
phase_4_ai.done_when: Summary and quiz work. hashchange guard blocks navigation. Answers final. SM-2 writes all fields including last_quiz_score as '4/5'. Offline errors show specific messages.

tasks: Complete dashboard: review queue, progress bars, stats.
Toast for all async ops.
Keyboard shortcuts: Esc (close panel), Ctrl+K (search focus).
Performance: 100+ nodes in all three graph modes.
README: browser support, npm install, folder structure, slugify examples, env vars, Cloudflare setup, Vite base, GitHub Pages, single-tab limitation, known limitations.

mandatory: NEVER store FileSystemHandle in sessionStorage/localStorage — idb-keyval only
NEVER hardcode AI model — env var with fallback
NEVER call Anthropic API directly — Worker only
NEVER start Phase 4 before ALL Phase 3 verifications pass
NEVER hardcode relation types as closed enum
NEVER send > 10 neighbors or > 200 chars/neighbor to AI
NEVER write .md without verifyYamlRoundTrip()
NEVER JSON.parse AI quiz output without parseQuizResponse()
NEVER call refreshGraph() after single write — use incremental updates
NEVER use fs.js or zip.js directly in components — storage.js only
NEVER show VITE_AI_PROXY_URL missing as silent failure — banner in detail panel
NEVER post BroadcastChannel PING before assigning onmessage
NEVER import store hooks inside store files — use getState() cross-store
NEVER fire onChange in NodeTargetSelector on every keystroke — only on select/blur
NEVER use navigateSafely() — hashchange guard + inline checks instead
NEVER pass _weight to sort neighbors — join with links array
NEVER include _handle/_filename in AI context payload — strip before sending
NEVER write color: null to frontmatter — use undefined so gray-matter omits it
NEVER write last_quiz_score as float — always '4/5' format string
ALWAYS call normalizeNode() on every parsed node
ALWAYS detect slug collisions in buildGraph()
ALWAYS use slugify() for IDs — never inline
ALWAYS normalize file extensions to lowercase
ALWAYS wrap computeStaticForceLayout in requestIdleCallback/setTimeout
ALWAYS use selectTopNodes() to select which 150 nodes to show in global view
ALWAYS cache globalLayoutCache — invalidate on node mutation
ALWAYS wrap GraphView in GraphErrorBoundary
ALWAYS disable React StrictMode — comment in main.jsx why
ALWAYS set Vite base to repo name
ALWAYS show non-dismissible orange banner in ZIP mode
ALWAYS fire beforeunload on zipDirty OR editorDirty
ALWAYS show scan counter (folder: two-pass, ZIP: single-pass)
ALWAYS update updatedAt on every save
ALWAYS rebuild Fuse index on nodes change (useEffect)
ALWAYS store rawContentCache for parse-failed files
ALWAYS catch TypeError in callAI() for captive-portal/restricted networks
ALWAYS refresh _handle after rename
ALWAYS update storage.js in Phase 1b (not optional)
ALWAYS define score = correctCount/questions.length before sm2()
ALWAYS disable quiz options immediately on first click
ALWAYS use useEffect to sync NodeTargetSelector inputText with external value
ALWAYS call refreshWarnings() after any operation that changes relations
ALWAYS use getNode(id, nodes) and getNeighborIds(id, depth, links) from graph.js
ALWAYS pin all package versions — no 'latest'
performance_rules: Global: d3AlphaDecay=1 d3VelocityDecay=1 cooldownTicks=0 fx/fy from cache
300 D3 ticks (150 if nodes > 100), wrapped in requestIdleCallback
Cache AI per node per session

sample_data.critical_note: IDs are exact slugify() outputs. 'TCP/IP' → 'tcpip'. color field OMITTED (not null) in all samples.

nodes.body:
## What is Docker?
Docker is a containerization platform that packages apps with their dependencies.

## Key Concepts
- **Image**: Read-only template
- **Container**: Running instance
- **Dockerfile**: Build script
- **Registry**: Image storage

## Common Commands
```bash
docker build -t my-app .
docker run -p 3000:3000 my-app
docker ps
```

## What is Kubernetes?
K8s is an open-source container orchestration platform.

## Key Concepts
- **Pod**: Smallest deployable unit
- **Node**: Worker machine
- **Cluster**: Set of nodes
- **Deployment**: Manages pod replicas
- **Service**: Stable network endpoint

## What is Linux?
Linux is an open-source Unix-like OS kernel by Linus Torvalds.

## Key Concepts
- Kernel, Shell, Filesystem, Processes, Permissions, Package managers

nodes.note: slugify('TCP/IP') === 'tcpip'. id and filename MUST be 'tcpip'.
nodes.body:
## What is TCP/IP?
TCP/IP is the foundational protocol suite of the internet.

## Layers
- Application, Transport, Internet, Network Access

## Protocols
- **TCP**: Reliable ordered delivery
- **UDP**: Fast, no guarantee
- **IP**: Addressing and routing

## What is HTTP?
HTTP is the application-layer protocol for web data transfer.

## Key Concepts
- **Methods**: GET POST PUT PATCH DELETE
- **Status codes**: 2xx 3xx 4xx 5xx
- **Headers**: Content-Type Authorization Cache-Control
- **HTTPS**: HTTP over TLS

lib/.graph.js: normalizeNode(), getNode(), getNeighborIds(), buildGraph(), buildLinks(), buildClusters(), refreshWarnings(), computeStaticForceLayout(), selectTopNodes()
lib/.fs.js: Two-pass folder scan. idb-keyval persistence. Extension normalization.
lib/.storage.js: Unified interface: readAllNodes, writeNode, deleteNode, renameNode, getMode, isZipDirty, exportZip. All components use ONLY this file.

src/.App.jsx: HashRouter root. hashchange quiz guard listener.
src/.main.jsx: StrictMode omitted — comment explains react-force-graph-2d reason.

project_structure.README.md: Browser support | npm install | slugify examples | env vars | Cloudflare setup | Vite base | GitHub Pages | single-tab warning | known limitations
